posts.name: Hank Victor
posts.cooked:
tweglobal.com

TWE announces completion of Château Lanessan acquisition - Treasury Wine Estates

Treasury Wine Estates Ltd (TWE), one of the world’s leading premium wine companies, today confirmed the completion of its majority acquisition of Château Lanessan, one of the oldest estates in Bordeaux, France.

Can’t wait for the 19 Crimes Bordeaux line extension.

link_counts.title: TWE announces completion of Château Lanessan acquisition - Treasury Wine Estates

posts.name: Julian Marshall
posts.cooked:
Thanks for the info!

Funnily enough, I was thinking about Lanessan earlier this week when posting about Bordeaux CBs.

I dropped in very briefly this summer and was struck by how rundown the estate was, from the potholed road to the château buildings themselves. It seemed just the sort of property in need of investment, but whether or not TWE are the right sort of investor I don’t know.

posts.name: Julian Marshall
posts.cooked: Laschenaye is included. Coming after Cambon la Pelouse and Belle Vue, that’s a lot of properties they have snapped up. Not sure it’s a good thing for consumers.

posts.name: Robert Alfert
posts.cooked: I did, and then I saw hope, perhaps they fire Eric Boissenot and Hubert de Bouard!

posts.name: Robert Alfert
posts.cooked:
Bolaire as well.

Have always enjoyed this quirky Cru, with its very heavy cut of Petit Verdot (up to 50%).

A Venn Diagram wine where @Jeff_Leve and Alfert both concur. He’s salvageable, Alfert all.

posts.name: John Glas
posts.cooked: 19 Crimes was one of my worst wines of the year a few years back. All label and juice is terrible.

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked: Quite right. The terroir has immense potential, and that potential is manifest in vintages such as '82, '59, but it needs a lot of investment. They currently have two tractors for 85 hectares of vines! If it were planted at 10,000 vines per hectare with high quality selections of Cabernet and farmed intelligently there’s no reason it shouldn’t embarrass many, many classified growths—if my intuitions for the quality of the site are anything to go by!

posts.name: Robert Alfert
posts.cooked: Popped another 2014 last night. Don’t need to go back to 82 and 59! Love this vintage of Lanessan, amassed three cases and wouldn’t mind grabbing more.

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked: It’s very nice, I agree, and the 2016 is even better; but based on the terroir I would say it could be competitive with some good third growths with the right investment.
